sectionDescription: We represent the different actors in the forestry chain in Uruguay with the objective of promoting sustainable development of the sector from the economic, social and environmental perspectives. We promote the development of plantations and the conservation and management of natural forests in the country. We contribute to improving commercial integration and positioning of the Uruguayan forestry industry in the world.
sanidadDescription: Forest protection is a matter of fundamental importance for the sustainable development of the sector. In this sense, the Forest Sanity Commission of SPF coordinates efforts among the different involved agents, which it considers key to obtain the benefits of the important investments made in implementing, developing and conserving the current and future forest heritage.
paifDescription: It is a national scope system, funded by member companies, that works each summer to achieve immediate detection of any fire hotspot that could threaten protected plantations. Once a hotspot is identified, a rapid and coordinated response is activated necessary to prevent its spread, through a quick and effective initial attack. The fundamental pillars of the system are: prevention, detection, coordination and dispatch, and combat.
laboralesDescription: SPF has legal advice on labor matters and a commission that works in this area, integrated with representatives of the member companies. Its main function is to develop guidelines for the tripartite negotiation as representative of the sector in the Salary Council, and to address other labor-related issues.
comunicacionDescription: Communication is a fundamental pillar in all business areas and sectors. From this area, annual communication plans are developed and digital tools, press actions, the Forest Review and institutional strategies are coordinated. The relationship with different actors and stakeholders of interest is also promoted, ensuring clear, coherent and aligned communication with the institution's objectives. The work of the area is cross-cutting to all areas of SPF, supporting and strengthening their initiatives.
